texto: The great opposition to reading is what I allow to fill my time instead of reading. To say we have no time to read is not really true; we simply have chosen to use our time for other things, or have allowed our time to be filled to the exclusion of reading. So don't add reading to your to-do list. Just stop doing the things that keep you from doing it. But read.
explicacao: Citação de James Emery White sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “The great opposition to reading is what I allow to fill my time instead of reading. To say we hav…” articula tempo, priorities, reading em registro de tempo, com relevância para contextos como reading e time.

frase_original: The great opposition to reading is what I allow to fill my time instead of reading. To say we have no time to read is not really true; we simply have chosen to use our time for other things, or have allowed our time to be filled to the exclusion of reading. So don't add reading to your to-do list. Just stop doing the things that keep you from doing it. But read.
